Props.text:
Drives system info, clocks, progress and icons with formulas, laid over your desktop wallpaper—
Sitting beneath ordinary windows: non-intrusive and non-topmost, quietly showing everything you care about.

Ctrl+Alt+→ / ←  Page      Ctrl+Alt+H  Show / Hide     Ctrl+Alt+Q  Quit
Right-click an empty desktop area to enter "Edit Mode" and start adding and arranging your widgets.
Turn to the next page—five minutes to learn everything Lumen can do →
Four keywords to understand how Lumen is organised

A complete workspace: all pages (with every widget) + per-page settings + global variables + user presets.
Create, switch, rename, export and import—like swapping your whole desktop setup.

Like slides—a profile holds up to 9 pages.
Use Ctrl+Alt+← / → to flip pages; each page has its own grid, background and widgets.

The widgets on a page, in six families: Text / Shape / Icon / Image / Progress / Container.
Drag to place; nine-grid anchors adapt to window size.

Wrap an expression in ＄...＄ to make a widget show live data.
E.g. type "Battery ＄bi(level)＄%" in text and it refreshes with the battery level.

Edit Mode
Drag, select and edit properties; the "Widget Tree + Property Panel" pops up automatically.
Right-click an empty desktop area to switch between the two modes.
Lumen v1.0   ·   User Manual   ·   Page 2 of 9
All shortcuts share the modifier  Ctrl + Alt
System tray icon: left-click = open Settings, right-click = menu (Settings / Show-Hide / Quit); right-click an empty overlay area = global menu.
Lumen v1.0   ·   User Manual   ·   Page 3 of 9
Meet every widget you can place on a page
Text, with embedded formulas for live data
Font icon, shown by code point
Local image, with several fit modes

Anchor: nine-grid (TopLeft … Center … BottomRight) sets the base position
Offset X/Y: pixel offset from the anchor; auto-repositions when the window changes
Opacity: 0 ~ 1        Rotation: -180° ~ +180°

Enter: None / Fade / Slide / Zoom / Drop
Loop: None / Pulse / Rotate / Blink / Float / Bounce
Default enter duration 400 ms, default loop duration 2000 ms; the loop follows automatically after enter.
Lumen v1.0   ·   User Manual   ·   Page 4 of 9
The key adjustable items per atom family

Text (formulas supported) · Colour · Size 1–400 · Font · Weight (Thin~ExtraBold)
Align · Line spacing · Word wrap · Shadow · Background colour · Padding

Shape: Rect / Ellipse / Line (rounded = Rect + corner radius)
Fill · Stroke · Stroke width · Corner radius · Dash (Solid/Dash/Dot) · Shadow

Glyph code point: hexadecimal, e.g. E974
Font (Segoe MDL2 Assets, etc.) · Size · Colour · Background circle · Shadow

Image path (png/jpg/…)
Fit: None / Uniform / Fill / UniformToFill · Corner radius · Placeholder background

Value 0–100 (formulas supported, default ＄bi(level)＄) · Style Bar / Ring
Colour · Background · Thickness · Show percentage

Layout: Overlap / Stack (vertical) / Series (horizontal)
Background · Corner radius · Stroke · Padding · Recursively nestable sub-widgets
Lumen v1.0   ·   User Manual   ·   Page 5 of 9
Use ＄...＄ to bring widgets to life

Wrap: enclose an expression in ＄...＄, e.g.   Battery ＄bi(level)＄%
Operators: + - * /   Compare > < >= <= = != ~= (use a single = for equality, ~= for regex match)   Logic & |
Variables: write gv(name) or a bare name inside a formula; write gv:name in a property box to bind a variable
Colours: #RGB / #RRGGBB / #AARRGGBB
df(fmt) time format · tf(secs) seconds to duration · ts([fmt]) timestamp · tu() UTC · tz() time zone
si(key) system metric · bi(key) battery · dp(px) DPI scale
if(c,a,b) condition · tc(cmd,txt[,n]) text processing · uc(t) uppercase · re(t,pat,rep) regex replace · fl(idx,…) get argument · rng([min,max]) random
gv(name[,def]) variable · mi(key) media info · mu(cmd) media control · ai([n]) apps · an(n) launch
Lumen v1.0   ·   User Manual   ·   Page 6 of 9
Live info formulas can read + reuse your settings

cpu · mem / memused / memtotal · diskfree / disktotal / diskp
netup / netdown · rwidth / rheight / density / dark
level battery % · plugged is plugged in · charging is charging

mi: title · artist · album · app · playing · pos · dur · avail
mu: play / pause / next / prev / stop
ai() number of apps · ai(n) name of the n-th app · an(n) launch the n-th (index starts at 1)
Global Variables (settings reused across widgets and pages)

Five types: Number · Text · Colour · Font · List
List: options separated by vertical bars (e.g.  Red|Green|Blue); the formula returns the current selection
Reference: gv(name) inside a formula; gv:name in a property box. Change it once and every reference updates—ideal for a theme colour.
Lumen v1.0   ·   User Manual   ·   Page 7 of 9
Click Actions (triggered by clicking a widget in Desktop Mode, 10 kinds)

None · Run app · Media control (play/pause/next/prev/stop) · Switch page (0-based, or +1 / -1)
Toggle edit mode · Open settings · Lock screen · Open URL · Run command (cmd /c) · Switch preset (preset name, or +1 / -1)

Tip: click actions fire only in Desktop Mode; in Edit Mode clicks are for selecting and dragging.
Triggers · Flows (auto-run a group of actions when conditions are met)

Condition: a boolean formula, e.g.   bi(level) < 20    or    mi(playing) = "Playing"
Mode: Once = fires once the instant the condition holds (can fire again after it drops); While = fires each cycle while it holds
Flow: an ordered group of actions run in sequence when the condition is met (steps can be moved up/down, added or removed)

Triggers are evaluated only in Desktop Mode.
Lumen v1.0   ·   User Manual   ·   Page 8 of 9
Wrapping up: reskin, switch profiles, then build your first page

Appearance preset: only swaps layers/grid/background, applies to all pages (e.g. one-tap Day/Night reskin)
Scene preset: a snapshot of a whole page (widgets included); applying it replaces the current page

Solid: #AARRGGBB      Image: a local image file
Formula: auto-wrapped in ＄＄ (e.g. gv(accent))      Variable: gv:name
One profile = all pages + widgets + variables + presets. Create / switch / rename / delete / export / import—carry your whole desktop with you.
Quick Start · Build your first page in five steps

① Right-click an empty desktop area → enter Edit Mode
② Right-click → Add Widget, then drag Text / Progress / Icon to where you want
③ Fill in a formula in Text or Progress, e.g.   ＄df(HH:mm)＄    or   ＄bi(level)＄
④ Right-click → exit Edit Mode, back to static Desktop Mode
⑤ Ctrl+Alt+← / → to flip pages; Ctrl+Alt+H to show or hide anytime
Lumen v1.0   ·   User Manual   ·   Page 9 of 9
